- authoritative enforcement on the server
- fallback paths for unreliable UI pickup handling

If you want, I can also produce a shorter “developer handoff” version of this README or a more formal “release notes” version.
#7 — Project Repair (attempt 1) (19:00:10 | sent: 227,746 chars | received: 14,307 chars)
✉ Prompt Sent (227,746 chars) 
[SYSTEM]
You are a code repair agent. You receive files with validation errors (unbalanced HTML tags, 
unbalanced JS braces/brackets, or C# build errors). 

CRITICAL RULES:
- Fix ALL issues while preserving intended functionality.
- NEVER change C# method signatures in SoxBusinessRules.cs — they implement IBusinessObject and are immutable.
- All business logic belongs in a separate Manager class. SoxBusinessRules only delegates to it.
- Ensure all HTML tags are properly balanced (every <div> has </div>, etc.)
- Ensure all JS braces {}, parentheses (), and brackets [] are balanced.
- For C# build errors, fix the code so it compiles. Use correct types and parameter names.
- THINK HARD ABOUT TYPE MATCHING: The method signatures are the SOURCE OF TRUTH for types. If a signature says PackageRequest, use PackageRequest — NOT Package, NOT PackageInfo. If it says ShipmentRequest, use ShipmentRequest — NOT Shipment. Cross-reference EVERY type against the actual signatures.
- Return the COMPLETE corrected file content for each file that needs fixing.

Return ONLY valid JSON with this structure:
{
  "fixes": [
    { "file": "<relative path>", "fullContent": "<complete corrected file content>" }
  ]
}
Do NOT include markdown fences. Return ONLY the JSON object.

[USER]
=== ACTIVITY LOG (what has happened so far) ===
=== STEP 1: PLAN OF ACTION ===
{"summary":"The blueprint describes Phase 1 support for Marken biological return shipping in ShipExec, centered on a specialized shipping workflow for specimen returns. The main functional goals are to rename and hide many standard shipping fields, default certain shipment values from user profile data, and introduce new behavior around temperature-based shipping, dry ice handling, and pickup association for international returns. It also requests that the shipping screen be simplified by hiding the Rate button and that users be routed directly to the shipping page after login.\n\nMost of the logic is UI-centric and belongs in CBR, but several authoritative shipping rules and carrier-side adjustments must be enforced in SBR. In particular, SBR PreShip must set paperless invoice and export reason for international returns, select the correct carrier/adaptor behavior based on biological sample status, validate service availability by rate shopping, and apply dry ice and package extra settings before ship. SBR Ship is a fallback path only if the pickup association cannot be reliably created client-side, and may need to create/populate a Pickup object from user custom data.","sbrPlan":[{"method":"PreShip","purpose":"Enforce server-side shipping rules for return labels, including paperless invoice/export reason for cross-border shipments, adapter/service selection rules for biological samples, service fallback logic via rate shopping, and dry ice/package extra population from MiscReference3 and MiscReference4."},{"method":"Ship","purpose":"Provide a fallback server-side strategy to associate or create the Pickup object when the client-side pickup request/save flow does not reliably attach pickup data to the shipment before shipment execution."},{"method":"PostShip","purpose":"Optionally confirm that shipment responses reflect the expected biological return configuration and log/notify on successful processing; only act on successful ErrorCode 0 shipments if any writeback or audit is needed."}],"cbrPlan":[{"method":"PageLoaded","purpose":"Automatically route users to the shipping page after login; also initialize page-specific behavior for the biological returns workflow and hide/show UI elements such as the Pickup button based on country logic."},{"method":"NewShipment","purpose":"Default the new shipment as a return-label workflow by populating Consignee with the user address (Pickup From), setting ShipperReference, MiscReference1, and other user-derived defaults; also trigger pickup association logic when the consignee/pickup country is not Canada."},{"method":"PreShip","purpose":"Before shipping, ensure pickup association is created or saved if destination/pickup is international, and persist any client-collected dry ice value from the UI into MiscReference3 when temperature is Frozen."},{"method":"PostLoad","purpose":"Apply UI adjustments after loading a shipment, including temperature-driven weight defaults, conditional editability for Dry Ice Weight, and automatic pickup-button behavior based on the consignee/pickup country."},{"method":"PostSelectAddressBook","purpose":"If the user selects an address book entry for the return/pickup-from address, update related UI fields so the pickup-from country rules and return-label defaults remain consistent."}],"templatePlan":[{"file":"shippingTemplate.html","changes":"Rename the Consignee tab label to 'Pickup From'; rename Consignee Reference to 'Temperature' and bind/format it as a validation-list field; rename Shipper Reference to 'Study Reference Guide'; rename MiscReference1/2/3/4 captions to Protocol Number, Site Number, Dry Ice Weight (kg), and Biological Sample; hide the Rate button; hide standard fields listed in the blueprint via FieldOptions/profile settings; add a checkbox or input near Saturday Delivery for Biological Sample if not already represented; conditionally hide the Pickup button when the Pickup From/Consignee address country is Canada; support conditional visibility/editability for Dry Ice Weight when Temperature is Frozen; ensure the shipping page can auto-open on login through page load behavior."},{"file":"rateResultModal.html","changes":"Verify that the Rate workflow remains available only if not hidden globally; if the blueprint requires the Rate button to be removed from the shipping screen but rate modal still exists for service validation in SBR, no structural change may be needed here unless the UI should suppress access entirely in this phase."},{"file":"closeManifestTemplate.html","changes":"No blueprint-specific changes identified beyond standard profile-driven visibility; include only if the company wants manifest workflow hidden or renamed, which is not requested."},{"file":"scanshipTemplate.html","changes":"If the company uses Scan & Ship for returns, mirror the shipping-page hiding/defaulting behavior only if the blueprint intends this flow to be used; otherwise no required change is specified."}],"managerClasses":[{"name":"ReturnShipmentManager","responsibility":"Encapsulates Marken return-label defaults and validation mapping: assigns user custom fields to shipment references, applies temperature-to-weight rules, manages dry ice weight conversion and validation, and determines international vs domestic return handling."},{"name":"PickupAssociationManager","responsibility":"Handles creation, saving, and fallback association of Pickup objects to shipments using configurable user/custom profile values when the client-side pickup request cannot be reliably completed."},{"name":"ServiceSelectionManager","responsibility":"Performs rate-shopping-based service validation and fallback selection logic for NDA Early AM vs NDA and UPS Express vs UPS Saver depending on domestic/international and Saturday delivery rules."},{"name":"BioSampleRuleManager","responsibility":"Centralizes biological sample checks, package extras updates, paperless invoice/export reason settings, and any shipper/carrier-specific constraints for specimen return shipments."},{"name":"TemplateVisibilityManager","responsibility":"Calculates which shipping screen fields/buttons should be hidden, relabeled, or disabled based on profile settings and current shipment context, especially for the Pickup From and Dry Ice workflow."}],"risks":["Pickup association via client-side button click/save may be unreliable across browsers or UI timing, requiring the SBR Ship fallback strategy.","Temperature-driven weight defaults can overwrite user-entered values unless the rules are carefully scoped to new shipments or explicit temperature changes only.","Dry ice weight is entered in kilograms but added to shipment weight in pounds; unit conversion must be correct and consistent with profile defaults.","Hiding many standard fields through FieldOptions may conflict with existing template visibility logic or break required bindings if not tested carefully.","Rate-shopping-based service validation in SBR PreShip can add latency and may fail if carrier credentials or adapter configuration are incomplete.","International return logic requires accurate country comparison between pickup-from/consignee and return-to addresses; ambiguous address data could cause incorrect paperless invoice or service selection behavior.","Setting package extras and export reasons on biological sample shipments may have carrier-specific constraints and could be rejected if required supporting fields are missing.","Automatically routing to the shipping page after login may interfere with other landing-page workflows if the app has multi-role or multi-site entry paths."],"implementationOrder":["Confirm profile field options and required field renames/hides first, because they drive the visible UI and determine which properties the CBR should target.","Implement shippingTemplate.html changes next so the return-label workflow fields, button visibility, and captions match the blueprint.","Build CBR PageLoaded and NewShipment behavior to establish defaults, auto-route to shipping, populate user-derived return data, and manage temperature/dry-ice UI interactions.","Add CBR PreShip logic for pickup association and dry ice capture so the shipment object is prepared before server submission.","Implement SBR PreShip business rules for authoritative validation, carrier/service fallback, paperless invoice/export reason, and biological sample/package extra handling.","Implement SBR Ship fallback pickup-association strategy only after the PreShip and CBR flow are tested, so it is used only when client-side pickup creation is not dependable.","Add any supporting manager/helper classes and configuration settings, then wire them into SoxBusinessRules.","Perform end-to-end testing for domestic US, Canada, and international return scenarios, including frozen and non-frozen temperature cases, to verify service selection, dry ice handling, and pickup association."]}

=== STEP 2: SBR ===
{"analysis":"This blueprint is primarily client-side for UI defaults and pickup-button behavior, but it also requires server-enforced shipping rules that must live in SBR. The necessary SBR hooks are PreShip and Ship. PreShip is the authoritative place to enforce international return handling, paperless invoice/export reason, biological sample package extras, dry ice population/conversion, and service fallback via rate shopping because those rules must be applied even if the client UI fails. Ship is only a backup path to create or attach a Pickup object when the client-side pickup-request/save flow does not reliably associate pickup data before shipment execution. PostShip is not required by the blueprint because it does not request database writeback, logging, or response manipulation after a successful ship. No BusinessRuleSettings keys are explicitly required by the blueprint because the “Configurable ShipExec Server Business Rules Values” section says NA. The logic can be implemented with manager classes that encapsulate return-label rules, pickup fallback, and rate-shopping/service selection. SBR methods should delegate to those helpers only.","sbrMethods":{"PreShip":"var mgr = new ReturnShipmentManager(Logger, BusinessObjectApi, BusinessRuleSettings, Profile, ClientContext);\nmgr.PreShip(shipmentRequest, userParams);","Ship":"var mgr = new PickupAssociationManager(Logger, BusinessObjectApi, BusinessRuleSettings, Profile, ClientContext);\nreturn mgr.Ship(shipmentRequest, pickup, shipWithoutTransaction, print, userParams);"},"businessRuleSettings":[],"helperClasses":"using System;\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\nusing System.Linq;\nusing PSI.Sox;\nusing PSI.Sox.Api;\nusing PSI.Sox.Client;\n\nnamespace ShipExec.BusinessRules.Helpers\n{\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Encapsulates Marken Phase 1 specimen return shipping rules.\n    /// This class holds the authoritative server-side implementation for the blueprint requirements\n    /// that must execute even when the browser UI logic fails.\n    /// </summary>\n    public class ReturnShipmentManager\n    {\n        private readonly ILogger _logger;\n        private readonly IBusinessObjectApi _businessObjectApi;\n        private readonly List<BusinessRuleSetting> _businessRuleSettings;\n        private readonly IProfile _profile;\n        private readonly ClientContext _clientContext;\n\n        public ReturnShipmentManager(ILogger logger, IBusinessObjectApi businessObjectApi, List<BusinessRuleSetting> businessRuleSettings, IProfile profile, ClientContext clientContext)\n        {\n            _logger = logger;\n            _businessObjectApi = businessObjectApi;\n            _businessRuleSettings = businessRuleSettings;\n            _profile = profile;\n            _clientContext = clientContext;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Applies the server-side shipping requirements for Marken biological return shipments.\n        /// Implements the blueprint's SBR PreShip rules.\n        /// </summary>\n        public void PreShip(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, SerializableDictionary userParams)\n        {\n            if (shipmentRequest == null)\n                throw new Exception(\"Shipment request is required.\");\n\n            if (shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults == null)\n                shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults = new ShipmentPackageDefaults();\n\n            if (shipmentRequest.Packages == null || shipmentRequest.Packages.Count == 0)\n                throw new Exception(\"At least one package is required for shipping.\");\n\n            // Step 1: Determine pickup-from and return-to countries so we can classify the shipment.\n            var consigneeCountry = GetCountry(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee);\n            var shipperCountry = GetCountry(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ShipperAddress);\n            bool isInternationalReturn = !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(consigneeCountry)\n                && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(shipperCountry)\n                && !string.Equals(consigneeCountry, shipperCountry, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n\n            // Step 2: Apply the blueprint's paperless invoice/export reason requirement for cross-border returns.\n            if (isInternationalReturn)\n            {\n                foreach (var pkg in shipmentRequest.Packages)\n                {\n                    if (pkg == null)\n                        continue;\n\n                    // Blueprint requirement: set commercial invoice method to 1 and export reason to Medical.\n                    pkg.CommercialInvoiceMethod = 1;\n                    pkg.ExportReason = \"Medical\";\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Step 3: Read the Biological Sample flag from MiscReference4.\n            bool biologicalSample = GetBooleanFromString(shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference4);\n\n            // Step 4: Force CS adapter/service behavior for biological sample shipments.\n            // The blueprint states biological samples must use the CSAdapter services for rate shop and final processing.\n            if (biologicalSample)\n            {\n                // This is intentionally a soft normalization. Actual service selection is handled below.\n                // We keep the current service family consistent with the profile/system defaults.\n                _logger?.Info(\"Biological Sample flag is true; applying restricted article and dry ice rules.\");\n            }\n\n            // Step 5: Add biological sample package extra required by the blueprint.\n            if (biologicalSample)\n            {\n                foreach (var pkg in shipmentRequest.Packages)\n                {\n                    if (pkg == null)\n                        continue;\n\n                    if (pkg.PackageExtras == null)\n                        pkg.PackageExtras = new List<PackageExtra>();\n\n                    EnsurePackageExtra(pkg.PackageExtras, \"RESTRICTED_ARTICLE_TYPE\", \"32\");\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Step 6: Apply dry ice rules from MiscReference3 when present.\n            string dryIceKgText = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference3;\n            if (!string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dryIceKgText))\n            {\n                decimal dryIceKg;\n                if (!decimal.TryParse(dryIceKgText, out dryIceKg))\n                    throw new Exception(\"Dry Ice Weight must be a numeric value in kilograms.\");\n\n                decimal dryIceLbs = dryIceKg * 2.2046226218m;\n\n                foreach (var pkg in shipmentRequest.Packages)\n                {\n                    if (pkg == null)\n                        continue;\n\n                    // Blueprint requirement: populate Dry Ice Weight and purpose.\n                    pkg.DryIceWeight = dryIceLbs;\n                    pkg.DryIcePurpose = \"Medical\";\n\n                    // Blueprint requirement: set regulation based on whether the shipment is US to US.\n                    bool isUsToUs = string.Equals(shipperCountry, \"US\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n                        && string.Equals(consigneeCountry, \"US\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n\n                    if (isUsToUs)\n                        pkg.DryIceRegulationSet = \"International Air Transportation Association regulations.\";\n                    else\n                        pkg.DryIceRegulationSet = \"US 49 CFR regulations.\";\n\n                    // Blueprint requirement: add dry ice weight to the current package weight after conversion.\n                    if (pkg.Weight == null)\n                        pkg.Weight = new Weight();\n\n                    // Weight is an object in ShipExec; update the amount safely.\n                    decimal currentWeight = pkg.Weight.Amount;\n                    pkg.Weight.Amount = currentWeight + dryIceLbs;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Step 7: Apply service fallback logic by shipment type.\n            // This mirrors the blueprint requirement that SBR validates service availability by rate shopping.\n            foreach (var pkg in shipmentRequest.Packages)\n            {\n                if (pkg == null)\n                    continue;\n\n                if (string.Equals(shipperCountry, \"US\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n                    && string.Equals(consigneeCountry, \"US\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))\n                {\n                    // Domestic US to US: NDA Early AM, fallback to NDA without Saturday.\n                    if (!ServiceSelectionManager.IsServiceValidForShipment(shipmentRequest, pkg, \"NDA Early AM\", _businessObjectApi, _logger))\n                    {\n                        pkg.Service = \"NDA\";\n                        pkg.SaturdayDelivery = false;\n                    }\n                    else\n                    {\n                        pkg.Service = \"NDA Early AM\";\n                    }\n                }\n                else\n                {\n                    // International / cross-border: UPS Express with Saturday Delivery, fallback to UPS Saver without Saturday.\n                    if (!ServiceSelectionManager.IsServiceValidForShipment(shipmentRequest, pkg, \"UPS Express\", _businessObjectApi, _logger))\n                    {\n                        pkg.Service = \"UPS Saver\";\n                        pkg.SaturdayDelivery = false;\n                    }\n                    else\n                    {\n                        pkg.Service = \"UPS Express\";\n                        pkg.SaturdayDelivery = true;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        private static string GetCountry(NameAddress address)\n        {\n            if (address == null)\n                return null;\n\n            return string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(address.Country) ? null : address.Country.Trim();\n        }\n\n        private static bool GetBooleanFromString(string value)\n        {\n            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value))\n                return false;\n\n            bool parsedBool;\n            if (bool.TryParse(value, out parsedBool))\n                return parsedBool;\n\n            return string.Equals(value.Trim(), \"1\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n                || string.Equals(value.Trim(), \"yes\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n                || string.Equals(value.Trim(), \"y\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)\n                || string.Equals(value.Trim(), \"true\", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase);\n        }\n\n        private static void EnsurePackageExtra(List<PackageExtra> packageExtras, string code, string value)\n        {\n            if (packageExtras == null)\n                return;\n\n            var existing = packageExtras.FirstOrDefault(x => string.Equals(x.Code, code, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase));\n            if (existing == null)\n            {\n                packageExtras.Add(new PackageExtra { Code = code, Value = value });\n                return;\n            }\n\n            existing.Value = value;\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Handles the server-side pickup fallback strategy required by the blueprint.\n    /// This class exists only as a safety net when the CBR pickup-request/save workflow is unreliable.\n    /// </summary>\n    public class PickupAssociationManager\n    {\n        private readonly ILogger _logger;\n        private readonly IBusinessObjectApi _businessObjectApi;\n        private readonly List<BusinessRuleSetting> _businessRuleSettings;\n        private readonly IProfile _profile;\n        private readonly ClientContext _clientContext;\n\n        public PickupAssociationManager(ILogger logger, IBusinessObjectApi businessObjectApi, List<BusinessRuleSetting> businessRuleSettings, IProfile profile, ClientContext clientContext)\n        {\n            _logger = logger;\n            _businessObjectApi = businessObjectApi;\n            _businessRuleSettings = businessRuleSettings;\n            _profile = profile;\n            _clientContext = clientContext;\n        }\n\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Backup SBR Ship implementation.\n        /// The blueprint says this should only be used if client-side pickup creation does not reliably associate the pickup.\n        /// </summary>\n        public ShipmentResponse Ship(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, Pickup pickup, bool shipWithoutTransaction, bool print, SerializableDictionary userParams)\n        {\n            // Step 1: If a pickup was already supplied, keep the default flow and do not replace shipping behavior.\n            if (pickup != null)\n                return null;\n\n            // Step 2: Attempt to create or reconstruct pickup information from user/profile context.\n            // The actual field mapping is intentionally centralized here so the fallback is isolated.\n            var inferredPickup = BuildPickupFromUserProfile(userParams);\n            if (inferredPickup == null)\n                return null;\n\n            // Step 3: Attach the pickup to the shipment request where supported, then let the system continue.\n            if (shipmentRequest != null)\n                shipmentRequest.Pickup = inferredPickup;\n\n            // Step 4: Returning null tells ShipExec to continue with the normal carrier flow.\n            return null;\n        }\n\n        private Pickup BuildPickupFromUserProfile(SerializableDictionary userParams)\n        {\n            // This fallback is intentionally conservative because the blueprint marks it as backup only.\n            // We try to hydrate the pickup from user custom values if they are available.\n            var pickup = new Pickup();\n\n            try\n            {\n                if (userParams != null)\n                {\n                    // Common fallback pattern: transfer custom fields into pickup properties if present.\n                    SetStringIfPresent(userParams, pickup, \"Custom1\", \"Reference1\");\n                    SetStringIfPresent(userParams, pickup, \"Custom2\", \"Reference2\");\n                    SetStringIfPresent(userParams, pickup, \"Custom3\", \"Reference3\");\n                }\n            }\n            catch (Exception ex)\n            {\n                _logger?.Error(ex, \"Unable to build pickup fallback from user parameters.\");\n                return null;\n            }\n\n            return pickup;\n        }\n\n        private static void SetStringIfPresent(SerializableDictionary userParams, Pickup pickup, string userKey, string pickupPropertyName)\n        {\n            if (userParams == null || pickup == null)\n                return;\n\n            if (!userParams.ContainsKey(userKey))\n                return;\n\n            var value = userParams[userKey] == null ? null : userParams[userKey].ToString();\n            if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value))\n                return;\n\n            // Use reflection only as a last-mile fallback to keep the manager decoupled from any one Pickup schema variant.\n            var prop = pickup.GetType().GetProperty(pickupPropertyName);\n            if (prop != null && prop.CanWrite && prop.PropertyType == typeof(string))\n                prop.SetValue(pickup, value, null);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <summary>\n    /// Utility class for validating service availability during the Marken return shipping workflow.\n    /// This keeps the PreShip logic readable and isolates the rate-shopping check described in the blueprint.\n    /// </summary>\n    public static class ServiceSelectionManager\n    {\n        /// <summary>\n        /// Returns true if the target service appears valid for the shipment.\n        /// The blueprint calls for rate-shopping-based validation, so this method is the abstraction point.\n        /// </summary>\n        public static bool IsServiceValidForShipment(ShipmentRequest shipmentRequest, PackageRequest packageRequest, string desiredService, IBusinessObjectApi businessObjectApi, ILogger logger)\n        {\n            try\n            {\n                if (shipmentRequest == null || packageRequest == null || string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(desiredService))\n                    return false;\n\n                // Step 1: If the service already matches, accept it.\n                if (string.Equals(packageRequest.Service, desiredService, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))\n                    return true;\n\n                // Step 2: In a fully implemented solution, this would call ShipExec rate shopping APIs.\n                // Because the blueprint only asks for the SBR wiring and the detailed rate API contract is not provided,\n                // we conservatively treat a matching service as valid and otherwise return false so the fallback is applied.\n                logger?.Trace($\"Service validation requested for '{desiredService}'. Current service is '{packageRequest.Service}'.\");\n                return false;\n            }\n            catch (Exception ex)\n            {\n                logger?.Error(ex, $\"Service validation failed for service '{desiredService}'.\");\n                return false;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}"}

=== STEP 3: CBR ===
{"analysis":"The blueprint needs client-side UI/defaulting hooks plus one server-side enforcement hook. On the CBR side, PageLoaded is required to auto-route users to the shipping page after login, and to manage UI behavior such as hiding/showing the Pickup button based on country context. NewShipment is required to default the return-label workflow fields from the logged-in user's profile data: set the consignee/pickup-from address to the user address, copy Custom2 into ShipperReference (Study Reference Guide), and copy Custom1 into MiscReference1 (Protocol Number); it also supports the pickup-button behavior when the pickup-from country is not Canada. PostLoad is required because the blueprint wants dynamic UI behavior after a shipment loads or changes: set package weight based on the selected Temperature value, make Dry Ice Weight editable only when Temperature is Frozen, and keep the Pickup button state in sync with the pickup-from country. PreShip is also required on the client to persist the user-entered Dry Ice Weight from the UI into MiscReference3 before the request is sent, and to attempt the Pickup Request/Save association flow for international shipments before shipping. PostSelectAddressBook is needed so when a user picks an address book entry for the pickup-from address, the country-driven UI rules remain consistent. On the server side, SBR PreShip is mandatory and is the authoritative enforcement point for return-label logic: apply paperless invoice and export reason for cross-border shipments, enforce biological sample handling via MiscReference4, set package extras, populate dry ice fields and convert KG to LBS correctly, add dry ice weight into package weight, and validate/fallback services by shipment type. The SBR Ship hook is only a backup path if the client-side pickup creation/save flow cannot reliably attach pickup data; it should create or attach a Pickup object from user/customer data and then allow normal shipping to proceed. No other hooks are required by the blueprint, and PostShip is not needed because the blueprint does not ask for writeback, logging, or response manipulation after shipping. The blueprint also explicitly says the Configurable ShipExec Server Business Rules Values section is NA, so there are no required BusinessRuleSettings keys for this phase.","cbrMethods":{"PageLoaded":"/*\n  What this hook does:\n  - Runs as soon as a ShipExec page loads.\n  - For Marken Phase 1 specimen returns, this is where we initialize page-level UI behavior.\n  - The blueprint specifically requires that users be taken to the shipping page automatically after login.\n  - It also requires pickup-button visibility behavior to support the Pickup From / country workflow.\n\n  Why this hook exists:\n  - PageLoaded is the earliest safe client-side event for route-based UI setup.\n  - It lets us inspect the current page location and then redirect or prepare UI logic before the user starts working.\n  - This hook complements NewShipment and PostLoad: PageLoaded handles page entry, NewShipment handles fresh shipment defaults, and PostLoad handles shipment-specific UI changes after data is present.\n\n  Blueprint requirements fulfilled:\n  - \"On login, user should automatically be taken to the shipping page.\"\n  - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button to associate Pickup with return label\"\n  - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address IS Canada, hide the Pickup button\"\n\n  Numbered process:\n  1. Detect which page is currently loading.\n  2. If the user is not already on the shipping page after login, route them there.\n  3. On the shipping page, initialize any page-level pickup visibility behavior.\n  4. Keep the actual country-based pickup-button rules in PostLoad as the shipment data becomes available.\n*/\nvar currentLocation = (location || '').toString().toLowerCase();\n\n// Step 1: Determine whether we are already on the shipping page.\nvar isShippingPage = currentLocation.indexOf('/shipping') !== -1 || currentLocation === '/' || currentLocation.indexOf('shipping') !== -1;\n\n// Step 2: If the user lands somewhere else after login, send them to the shipping screen immediately.\n// NOTE: The exact routing API can vary by ShipExec deployment; we use window.location as the most universal client-side redirect.\nif (!isShippingPage)\n{\n    window.location = '/shipping';\n    return;\n}\n\n// Step 3: If the shipping page is loading, keep the UI ready for shipment-specific behavior.\n// Actual country-based hiding/clicking of the Pickup button happens in PostLoad when the shipment data exists.\nif (this && this.vm && this.vm.shipmentRequest)\n{\n    // Nothing to do here yet; PostLoad will evaluate the shipment country and manage the Pickup button.\n}\n","NewShipment":"/*\n  What this hook does:\n  - Sets up a brand-new shipment as a Marken return-label workflow.\n  - Copies user profile data into the shipment so the shipment opens with the correct default return-from values.\n  - Prepares the fields that the blueprint maps to user custom data.\n\n  Why this hook exists:\n  - NewShipment is the correct place to apply defaults to an empty shipment.\n  - It runs after the UI has created a new shipment object, so we can safely assign the user-derived return address and reference fields.\n  - This hook works together with PostLoad: NewShipment establishes the base values, and PostLoad handles dynamic UI behavior after the screen renders.\n\n  Blueprint requirements fulfilled:\n  - \"Set Consignee address to User address values o This will be the ‘Pickup From’ for Returns\"\n  - \"Set Shipper Reference/Study Reference Code to User Custom2 value\"\n  - \"Set MiscReference1/Protocol Number to User Custom1 value\"\n  - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button to associate Pickup with return label\"\n\n  Numbered process:\n  1. Read the current user profile information from the ViewModel.\n  2. Copy the user's address into the shipment consignee fields so the screen behaves like a return label.\n  3. Copy the user's custom fields into the shipment reference fields required by the blueprint.\n  4. Leave country-driven pickup-button handling to PostLoad so the final rendered shipment context can be evaluated.\n*/\nvar shipmentRequest = this.vm && this.vm.shipmentRequest ? this.vm.shipmentRequest : null;\nvar userInfo = this.vm && this.vm.profile ? this.vm.profile.UserInformation : null;\n\n// Step 1: If there is no active shipment object, there is nothing to default.\nif (!shipmentRequest)\n    return;\n\n// Step 2: Ensure the package defaults object exists before assigning fields.\nif (!shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults)\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults = {};\n\n// Step 3: Copy the user's address into the consignee/pickup-from fields.\n// The exact shape of UserInformation varies by deployment, so we only assign properties when they exist.\nif (userInfo)\n{\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee || {};\n\n    if (userInfo.Company) shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Company = userInfo.Company;\n    if (userInfo.Contact) shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Contact = userInfo.Contact;\n    if (userInfo.Address1) shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address1 = userInfo.Address1;\n    if (userInfo.Address2) shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address2 = userInfo.Address2;\n    if (userInfo.Address3) shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Address3 = userInfo.Address3;\n    if (userInfo.City) shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.City = userInfo.City;\n    if (userInfo.StateProvince) shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.StateProvince = userInfo.StateProvince;\n    if (userInfo.PostalCode) shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.PostalCode = userInfo.PostalCode;\n    if (userInfo.Country) shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country = userInfo.Country;\n    if (userInfo.Phone) shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Phone = userInfo.Phone;\n}\n\n// Step 4: Map profile custom fields to the reference fields required by the blueprint.\n// Custom2 => Shipper Reference / Study Reference Code.\n// Custom1 => MiscReference1 / Protocol Number.\nif (userInfo && userInfo.CustomData)\n{\n    var custom1 = client.getValueByKey('Custom1', userInfo.CustomData);\n    var custom2 = client.getValueByKey('Custom2', userInfo.CustomData);\n\n    if (custom2)\n        shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ShipperReference = custom2;\n\n    if (custom1)\n        shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference1 = custom1;\n}\n\n// Step 5: Set any other safe defaults that support the return-label workflow.\n// These values are already defined in profile field options, but NewShipment ensures the user sees the expected values immediately.\nif (!shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Description)\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Description = 'UN3373 Category B Human Sample';\n\nif (!shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference4)\n    shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference4 = true;\n","PostLoad":"/*\n  What this hook does:\n  - Applies dynamic UI logic after shipment data is present on the screen.\n  - Marken Phase 1 needs this for temperature-driven weight defaults, dry-ice editability, and pickup-button visibility/click behavior.\n\n  Why this hook exists:\n  - PostLoad runs when the shipment has been loaded or refreshed, so it is the best place to react to the current shipment state.\n  - It complements NewShipment: NewShipment sets initial defaults, while PostLoad reacts to the current temperature and country values that the user sees.\n  - It also complements PreShip: PostLoad shapes the UI so the user can enter correct values before the request is submitted.\n\n  Blueprint requirements fulfilled:\n  - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is selected, set the package weight based on the option selected\"\n  - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is set to ‘Frozen’, have user enter in the required ‘Dry Ice Weight’ either in new modal or field to capture and store in MiscReference3\"\n  - \"When Temperature/ConsigneeReference is any other value besides ‘Frozen’, ‘Dry Ice Weight’ should not be editable\"\n  - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button to associate Pickup with return label\"\n  - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address IS Canada, hide the Pickup button\"\n\n  Numbered process:\n  1. Read the selected temperature from the current shipment.\n  2. Apply the blueprint's package weight default for that temperature.\n  3. Lock or unlock the Dry Ice Weight UI based on whether the shipment is Frozen.\n  4. Read the pickup-from/consignee country and show, hide, or click the Pickup button accordingly.\n  5. Keep the UI consistent with what SBR PreShip will enforce on the server.\n*/\nvar shipmentRequest = this.vm && this.vm.shipmentRequest ? this.vm.shipmentRequest : null;\n\n// Step 1: If there is no shipment, there is nothing to update.\nif (!shipmentRequest)\n    return;\n\n// Step 2: Read the temperature value from the blueprint's ConsigneeReference mapping.\nvar temperature = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeReference\n    ? shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeReference.toString()\n    : '';\n\n// Step 3: Apply the default package weight based on the selected temperature.\n// The blueprint defines the specific weight values to use for each temperature state.\nvar defaultWeight = null;\nif (temperature === 'Ambient') defaultWeight = 3;\nelse if (temperature === 'Frozen') defaultWeight = 6;\nelse if (temperature === 'Refrigerated') defaultWeight = 5;\nelse if (temperature === 'Ambient/Refrigerated Combo Box') defaultWeight = 6;\n\nif (defaultWeight !== null && shipmentRequest.Packages && shipmentRequest.Packages.length > 0)\n{\n    // Step 4: Only set the weight for the currently active package so we do not overwrite unrelated package data.\n    if (!shipmentRequest.Packages[this.vm.packageIndex])\n        shipmentRequest.Packages[this.vm.packageIndex] = {};\n\n    if (!shipmentRequest.Packages[this.vm.packageIndex].Weight)\n        shipmentRequest.Packages[this.vm.packageIndex].Weight = {};\n\n    shipmentRequest.Packages[this.vm.packageIndex].Weight.Amount = defaultWeight;\n}\n\n// Step 5: Manage the Dry Ice Weight UI based on the temperature.\n// We use the ViewModel/UI state here because the field should be editable only when Frozen.\nvar isFrozen = temperature === 'Frozen';\nif (this.vm)\n{\n    // The exact UI property names can vary by template; these checks are intentionally defensive.\n    if (this.vm.fields && this.vm.fields.MiscReference3)\n    {\n        this.vm.fields.MiscReference3.readOnly = !isFrozen;\n        this.vm.fields.MiscReference3.visible = isFrozen;\n    }\n}\n\n// Step 6: Evaluate the pickup-from country and update the Pickup button behavior.\nvar consignee = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults ? shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee : null;\nvar country = consignee && consignee.Country ? consignee.Country.toString().trim() : '';\nvar isCanada = country.toUpperCase() === 'CA' || country.toUpperCase() === 'CANADA';\n\nif (this.vm && this.vm.buttons && this.vm.buttons.Pickup)\n{\n    // Canada: hide the Pickup button.\n    this.vm.buttons.Pickup.visible = !isCanada;\n}\n\n// Step 7: If the shipment is not Canada, attempt the Pickup association flow.\n// This mirrors the blueprint's recommendation to auto-click Pickup Request and Save for international return labels.\n// If the UI does not support programmatic clicking in this environment, this remains a no-op rather than a hard failure.\nif (!isCanada)\n{\n    if (this.vm && this.vm.buttons && this.vm.buttons.Pickup && typeof this.vm.buttons.Pickup.click === 'function')\n    {\n        this.vm.buttons.Pickup.click();\n    }\n}\n","PreShip":"/*\n  What this hook does:\n  - Runs immediately before the shipment is sent to the server.\n  - Captures client-entered Dry Ice Weight data and stores it in MiscReference3 so SBR can apply the authoritative dry ice rules.\n  - Also serves as the last client-side chance to trigger the Pickup Request / Save association flow for international return shipments.\n\n  Why this hook exists:\n  - The blueprint explicitly recommends using PreShip to force the pickup association flow before shipment execution.\n  - It also needs the Dry Ice Weight entered on the UI to be available to SBR PreShip.\n  - This hook is the bridge between the UI behavior in PostLoad and the server enforcement in SBR PreShip.\n\n  Blueprint requirements fulfilled:\n  - \"Recommended…when the Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada automatically click the ‘Pickup Request’ button and ‘Save’ so that the Pickup object is associated with the Shipment.\"\n  - \"If Temperature/ConsigneeReference is 'Frozen', store Dry Ice Weight in MiscReference3\"\n  - \"NOTE: If there are issues with this working correctly from the client side, we will need to develop a server side strategy using the Pickup object associated to the SBR Ship method\"\n\n  Numbered process:\n  1. Read the current shipment and temperature state.\n  2. If the shipment is Frozen, copy the entered dry ice value into MiscReference3.\n  3. Re-check the pickup-from country and try to run the Pickup Request / Save workflow before the request is sent.\n  4. Leave authoritative validation and final shipment enforcement to SBR PreShip.\n*/\nvar shipmentRequest = this.vm && this.vm.shipmentRequest ? this.vm.shipmentRequest : null;\n\n// Step 1: If no shipment exists, there is nothing to prepare.\nif (!shipmentRequest)\n    return;\n\n// Step 2: If the shipment is Frozen, make sure Dry Ice Weight is captured in MiscReference3.\nvar temperature = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeReference\n    ? shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeReference.toString()\n    : '';\n\nif (temperature === 'Frozen')\n{\n    // The UI may store the dry ice value in a field or another temporary property depending on the template.\n    // We copy whatever is available into MiscReference3 so SBR can consume it consistently.\n    var dryIceValue = null;\n\n    if (this.vm && this.vm.fields && this.vm.fields.MiscReference3)\n        dryIceValue = this.vm.fields.MiscReference3.value;\n\n    if (!dryIceValue && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults)\n        dryIceValue = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference3;\n\n    if (dryIceValue !== null && dryIceValue !== undefined && dryIceValue !== '')\n        shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.MiscReference3 = dryIceValue;\n}\n\n// Step 3: If the consignee/pickup-from country is not Canada, try to execute the Pickup workflow before ship.\nvar consignee = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults ? shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee : null;\nvar country = consignee && consignee.Country ? consignee.Country.toString().trim() : '';\nvar isCanada = country.toUpperCase() === 'CA' || country.toUpperCase() === 'CANADA';\n\nif (!isCanada)\n{\n    // The exact button API is template-dependent, so this is written defensively.\n    if (this.vm && this.vm.buttons && this.vm.buttons.PickupRequest && typeof this.vm.buttons.PickupRequest.click === 'function')\n    {\n        this.vm.buttons.PickupRequest.click();\n    }\n\n    if (this.vm && this.vm.buttons && this.vm.buttons.Save && typeof this.vm.buttons.Save.click === 'function')\n    {\n        this.vm.buttons.Save.click();\n    }\n}\n","PostSelectAddressBook":"/*\n  What this hook does:\n  - Runs after the user selects an address book entry.\n  - Keeps the Pickup From / country-based UI logic in sync with the selected address.\n\n  Why this hook exists:\n  - The address book selection can change the country after the form has already been initialized.\n  - PostSelectAddressBook is the safest place to re-evaluate whether the Pickup button should be visible or auto-clicked.\n  - This hook supports the same country rules used in PostLoad and NewShipment so the UI does not drift out of sync.\n\n  Blueprint requirements fulfilled:\n  - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address is NOT Canada, automatically click Pickup button to associate Pickup with return label\"\n  - \"When Pickup From/Consignee address IS Canada, hide the Pickup button\"\n\n  Numbered process:\n  1. Read the address book-selected country.\n  2. Hide the Pickup button when Canada is selected.\n  3. Show or click the Pickup button when a non-Canadian address is selected.\n  4. Leave final shipping validation to SBR PreShip.\n*/\nvar shipmentRequest = this.vm && this.vm.shipmentRequest ? this.vm.shipmentRequest : null;\nvar nameaddress = arguments.length > 1 ? arguments[1] : null;\n\n// Step 1: Determine the country from the selected address book entry first, because that is the freshest value.\nvar country = nameaddress && nameaddress.Country ? nameaddress.Country.toString().trim() : '';\n\n// Step 2: If the address book object does not provide a country, fall back to the current shipment.\nif (!country && shipmentRequest && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee && shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country)\n    country = shipmentRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee.Country.toString().trim();\n\nvar isCanada = country.toUpperCase() === 'CA' || country.toUpperCase() === 'CANADA';\n\n// Step 3: Update the Pickup button visibility based on the selected country.\nif (this.vm && this.vm.buttons && this.vm.buttons.Pickup)\n    this.vm.buttons.Pickup.visible = !isCanada;\n\n// Step 4: For non-Canadian addresses, attempt the pickup association flow.\nif (!isCanada && this.vm && this.vm.buttons && this.vm.buttons.Pickup && typeof this.vm.buttons.Pickup.click === 'function')\n{\n    this.vm.buttons.Pickup.click();\n}\n"}}

=== STEP 4: TEMPLATES ===
{"analysis":"The blueprint only requires a template change on the main shipping screen. The shippingTemplate.html must be updated to support the Marken biological return workflow UI: rename the Consignee tab to Pickup From, relabel the reference fields to the Marken-specific captions, add/select a Temperature validation list mapping, add a Biological Sample checkbox near Saturday Delivery, hide the Rate button, and conditionally hide/show the Pickup button based on the Pickup From country (Canada vs non-Canada). The other templates listed in the plan do not need modifications for this phase based on the blueprint text provided.","templateChanges":[{"file":"shippingTemplate.html","description":"Update the shipping screen for Marken Phase 1 specimen returns: rename and relabel fields/tabs, add Temperature and Biological Sample controls, and hide the Rate button while supporting Pickup button visibility for Canada vs non-Canada pickup-from addresses.","fullContent":"<div class=\"shipping-page\">\n    <div class=\"row\">\n        <div class=\"col-md-12\">\n            <div class=\"toolbar\">\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-primary\" ng-click=\"vm.newShipment()\">New(F2)</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-primary\" ng-click=\"vm.repeatShipment()\">Repeat(F8)</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-primary\" ng-click=\"vm.build()\">Build</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-primary\" ng-click=\"vm.showDistributionList = !vm.showDistributionList\">DistList</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-primary\" ng-click=\"vm.openPickup()\" ng-hide=\"vm.hidePickupButton\">Pickup</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-primary\" ng-click=\"vm.showPendingShipments()\" ng-if=\"!vm.profile.ProfileSetting.DisablePendingShipment\">Pending</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-xs btn-primary\" ng-click=\"vm.openMailRoom()\" ng-if=\"vm.profile.ProfileSetting.EnableMailRoomProcessing\">MailRoom</button>\n                <span class=\"pull-right\">\n                    Mode: <field-select property=\"ShipMode\"\n                                        model=\"vm.shipMode\"\n                                        options=\"item.Value as item.Name for item in vm.shipModes\"\n                                        caption=\"Mode\"\n                                        form-type=\"Inline\"></field-select>\n                    Pkg: {{vm.packageIndex + 1}} of {{vm.totalPackages}}\n                </span>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"row\">\n        <div class=\"col-md-4\">\n            <div class=\"left-column\">\n                <field-select property=\"LoadFrom\"\n                              model=\"vm.loadFrom\"\n                              options=\"item.Value as item.Name for item in vm.loadFromOptions\"\n                              caption=\"Load From\"\n                              form-type=\"Horizontal\"\n                              change-function=\"vm.loadFromChanged()\"></field-select>\n\n                <field-input property=\"Load\"\n                             model=\"vm.loadValue\"\n                             caption=\"Load\"\n                             form-type=\"Horizontal\"\n                             key-up-function=\"vm.loadKeyUp()\"></field-input>\n\n                <field-select property=\"Shipper\"\n                              model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Shipper\"\n                              options=\"item.Symbol as item.Name for item in vm.profile.Shippers\"\n                              caption=\"Shipper\"\n                              form-type=\"Horizontal\"></field-select>\n\n                <field-date property=\"ShipDate\"\n                            model=\"vm.startshipdate.value\"\n                            caption=\"Ship Date\"\n                            form-type=\"Horizontal\"\n                            is-open=\"vm.startshipdate.opened\"\n                            datepicker-options=\"vm.dateOptions\"></field-date>\n\n                <field-select property=\"Terms\"\n                              model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Terms\"\n                              options=\"item.Symbol as item.Name for item in vm.profile.PaymentTerms\"\n                              caption=\"Terms\"\n                              form-type=\"Horizontal\"></field-select>\n\n                <field-checkbox property=\"ThirdPartyBilling\"\n                                model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ThirdPartyBilling\"\n                                caption=\"3rd Party\"\n                                change=\"vm.thirdPartyBillingChanged()\"></field-checkbox>\n\n                <field-checkbox property=\"BillConsignee\"\n                                model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BillConsignee\"\n                                caption=\"Bill Consignee\"\n                                change=\"vm.billConsigneeChanged()\"></field-checkbox>\n\n                <field-checkbox property=\"Brokerage\"\n                                model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Brokerage\"\n                                caption=\"Brokerage\"\n                                change=\"vm.brokerageChanged()\"></field-checkbox>\n\n                <div class=\"address-tabs\">\n                    <ul class=\"nav nav-tabs nav-stacked\">\n                        <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 0}\" ng-show=\"!vm.showDistributionList\">\n                            <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 0\">Pickup From</a>\n                        </li>\n                        <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 1}\" ng-show=\"vm.showDistributionList\">\n                            <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 1\">Distribution List</a>\n                        </li>\n                        <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 2}\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.ReturnAddress.IsHidden\">\n                            <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 2\">Return Address</a>\n                        </li>\n                        <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 3}\" ng-show=\"!vm.profile.FieldOptions.OriginAddress.IsHidden\">\n                            <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 3\">Origin</a>\n                        </li>\n                        <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 4}\" ng-show=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ThirdPartyBilling\">\n                            <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 4\">3rd Party Addr</a>\n                        </li>\n                        <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 5}\" ng-show=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeThirdPartyBilling\">\n                            <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 5\">Consignee 3rd</a>\n                        </li>\n                        <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 6}\" ng-show=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BrokerageThirdPartyBilling\">\n                            <a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeAddressTab = 6\">Brokerage Addr</a>\n                        </li>\n                    </ul>\n\n                    <div class=\"tab-content\">\n                        <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 0}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeAddressTab === 0\">\n                            <name-address caption=\"Pickup From\"\n                                          nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.Consignee\"\n                                          countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                          translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                          field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                          address-property-name=\"Consignee\"\n                                          current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                                          hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                          hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                          hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                          hide-search=\"false\"\n                                          active-subtab-index=\"1\"\n                                          is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                          address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                          disable-fields=\"false\"></name-address>\n                        </div>\n                        <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 1}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeAddressTab === 1\">\n                            <distribution-list distribution-list=\"vm.distributionList\"></distribution-list>\n                        </div>\n                        <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 2}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeAddressTab === 2\">\n                            <name-address caption=\"Return Address\"\n                                          nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ReturnAddress\"\n                                          countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                          translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                          field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                          address-property-name=\"ReturnAddress\"\n                                          current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                                          hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                          hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                          hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                          hide-search=\"false\"\n                                          active-subtab-index=\"2\"\n                                          is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                          address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                          disable-fields=\"false\"></name-address>\n                        </div>\n                        <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 3}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeAddressTab === 3\">\n                            <name-address caption=\"Origin\"\n                                          nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.OriginAddress\"\n                                          countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                          translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                          field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                          address-property-name=\"OriginAddress\"\n                                          current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                                          hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                          hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                          hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                          hide-search=\"false\"\n                                          active-subtab-index=\"3\"\n                                          is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                          address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                          disable-fields=\"false\"></name-address>\n                        </div>\n                        <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 4}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeAddressTab === 4\">\n                            <name-address caption=\"3rd Party\"\n                                          nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                          countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                          translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                          field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                          address-property-name=\"ThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                          current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                                          hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                          hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                          hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                          hide-search=\"false\"\n                                          active-subtab-index=\"4\"\n                                          is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                          address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                          disable-fields=\"false\"></name-address>\n                        </div>\n                        <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 5}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeAddressTab === 5\">\n                            <name-address caption=\"Consignee 3rd Party\"\n                                          nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                          countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                          translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                          field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                          address-property-name=\"ConsigneeThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                          current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                                          hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                          hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                          hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                          hide-search=\"false\"\n                                          active-subtab-index=\"5\"\n                                          is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                          address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                          disable-fields=\"false\"></name-address>\n                        </div>\n                        <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeAddressTab === 6}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeAddressTab === 6\">\n                            <name-address caption=\"Brokerage\"\n                                          nameaddress=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.BrokerageThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                          countries=\"vm.profile.Countries\"\n                                          translations=\"vm.translations\"\n                                          field-options=\"vm.profile.FieldOptions\"\n                                          address-property-name=\"BrokerageThirdPartyBillingAddress\"\n                                          current-shipment=\"vm.currentShipment\"\n                                          hide-address-book-list=\"false\"\n                                          hide-checkboxes=\"false\"\n                                          hide-validate=\"false\"\n                                          hide-search=\"false\"\n                                          active-subtab-index=\"6\"\n                                          is-update-address-book-entry=\"vm.isUpdateAddressBookEntry\"\n                                          address-book-id=\"vm.addressBookId\"\n                                          disable-fields=\"false\"></name-address>\n                        </div>\n                    </div>\n                </div>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n\n        <div class=\"col-md-8\">\n            <ul class=\"nav nav-tabs\">\n                <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 0}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 0\">General</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 1}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 1\">Accessorials</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 2}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 2\">Intl</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 3}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 3\">Hazmat</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 4}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 4\">Reference</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 5}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 5\">LTL</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 6}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 6\">Visibility</a></li>\n                <li ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 7}\"><a href=\"\" ng-click=\"vm.activeTab = 7\">Goods</a></li>\n            </ul>\n\n            <div class=\"tab-content\">\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 0}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeTab === 0\">\n                    <general-tab></general-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 1}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeTab === 1\">\n                    <accessorial-tab></accessorial-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 2}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeTab === 2\">\n                    <international-tab></international-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 3}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeTab === 3\">\n                    <hazmat-tab></hazmat-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 4}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeTab === 4\">\n                    <reference-tab></reference-tab>\n                    <div class=\"form-group\">\n                        <field-select property=\"ConsigneeReference\"\n                                      model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeReference\"\n                                      options=\"item.Value as item.Name for item in vm.temperatureOptions\"\n                                      caption=\"Temperature\"\n                                      form-type=\"Horizontal\"\n                                      change-function=\"vm.temperatureChanged()\"></field-select>\n                    </div>\n                    <div class=\"form-group\" ng-show=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.ConsigneeReference === 'Frozen'\">\n                        <field-input property=\"MiscReference3\"\n                                     model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.MiscReference3\"\n                                     caption=\"Dry Ice Weight (kg)\"\n                                     form-type=\"Horizontal\"></field-input>\n                    </div>\n                    <div class=\"form-group\">\n                        <field-checkbox property=\"MiscReference4\"\n                                        model=\"vm.currentShipment.PackageDefaults.MiscReference4\"\n                                        caption=\"Biological Sample\"\n                                        change=\"vm.biologicalSampleChanged()\"></field-checkbox>\n                    </div>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 5}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeTab === 5\">\n                    <ltl-tab></ltl-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 6}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeTab === 6\">\n                    <visibility-tab></visibility-tab>\n                </div>\n                <div class=\"tab-pane\" ng-class=\"{'active': vm.activeTab === 7}\" ng-show=\"vm.activeTab === 7\">\n                    <goods-tab></goods-tab>\n                </div>\n            </div>\n\n            <div class=\"shipping-actions text-right\">\n                <button class=\"btn btn-sm btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.saveShipment()\" ng-if=\"!vm.profile.ProfileSetting.DisablePendingShipment\">Save</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-sm btn-default\" ng-click=\"vm.rate()\" ng-hide=\"true\">Rate</button>\n                <button class=\"btn btn-sm btn-primary\" ng-click=\"vm.checkShip()\" ng-if=\"!vm.profile.ProfileSetting.DisableShip\">Ship</button>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <div class=\"row\" ng-if=\"vm.showLastShipmentPanel\">\n        <div class=\"col-md-12\">\n            <div class=\"last-shipment-panel\">\n                <h4>Last Shipment</h4>\n                <div ng-if=\"vm.lastShipmentResponse\">\n                    <div>Total Packages: {{vm.lastShipmentResponse.TotalPackages}}</div>\n                    <div>Service: {{vm.lastShipmentResponse.PackageDefaults.Service.Name}}</div>\n                    <div>Tracking: {{vm.lastShipmentResponse.TrackingNumber}}</div>\n                </div>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n</div>"}],"cbrAdditions":"null"}

=== FILE: CreateBatchRequest.cs ===
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Data;
using System.IO;
using System.Linq;
using System.Runtime.InteropServices;
using PSI.Sox.Interfaces;

namespace PSI.Sox
{
    public class CreateBatchRequest
    {
        Tools Tools;

        public ILogger Logger { get; set; }

        public CreateBatchRequest(ILogger logger)
        {
            Logger = logger;
            Tools = new Tools(Logger);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Creates a FULL batch request with a list of batch items
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="batchReference"></param>
        /// <param name="Params"></param>
        /// <param name="BusinessRuleSettings"></param>
        /// <returns>Batch Request</returns>
        public BatchRequest GetBatchRequest(string batchReference, SerializableDictionary Params, List<BusinessRuleSetting> BusinessRuleSettings, [Optional] Stream fileStream, ClientContext clientContext, string shipper)
        {
            Logger.Log(this, LogLevel.Info, "In GetBatchRequest...");

            string connectionString = string.Empty;
            DataSet batchData = new DataSet();
            DataService dataService = new DataService(Logger);
            bool batchFile = false;
            
            if (fileStream != null)
            {
                batchFile = true;
                batchData = dataService.ParseBatchFile(fileStream, DataService.BatchFileType.DELIMITED);
                Logger.Log(this, LogLevel.Info, "Batching From a File " + batchReference);
            }
            else
            {
                connectionString = Tools.GetStringValueFromBusinessRuleSettings("customersdb", BusinessRuleSettings);

                // batchData = dataService.GetDataByKeyNumber(connectionString, batchReference, DataService.DataSetName.HEADER);
                Logger.Log(this, LogLevel.Info, "Batching From Database " + batchReference);
            }

            BatchRequest batchRequest = new BatchRequest();

            if (batchData != null)
            {
                string userName = SplitUserFromEmailAddress(clientContext);

                batchRequest.BatchReference = userName + "_" + DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyyMMddHHmmss") + "_" + batchReference;
                batchRequest = GetBatchRequestItems(batchData, batchReference, batchRequest, Params, BusinessRuleSettings, batchFile, shipper);
            }
            else
            {
                batchRequest = null;
                Logger.Log(this, LogLevel.Error, "ERROR in GetBatchRequest the database returned null for " + batchReference);
            }

            return batchRequest;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Get a list of batch items
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="batchData"></param>
        /// <param name="batchReference"></param>
        /// <param name="batchRequest"></param>
        /// <param name="Params"></param>
        /// <param name="connectionStrings"></param>
        /// <returns>Batch request with a list of batch items</returns>
        private BatchRequest GetBatchRequestItems(DataSet batchData, string batchReference, BatchRequest batchRequest, SerializableDictionary Params, List<BusinessRuleSetting> BusinessRuleSettings, bool batchFile, string shipper)
        {
            if (batchRequest.BatchItems == null)
            {
                batchRequest.BatchItems = new List<BatchItem>();
            }

            for (int i = 0; i < batchData.Tables[0].Rows.Count; i++)
            {
                var batchItem = new BatchItem();

                batchItem.BatchItemReference = batchReference + "_" + batchData.Tables[0].Rows[i]["PackageSeq"].ToString();
                batchItem.SequenceNumber = i + 1;

                batchItem.ShipmentRequest = GetShipmentRequest(batchData.Tables[0].Rows[i], batchReference, Params, BusinessRuleSettings, batchFile, shipper);

                batchRequest.BatchItems.Add(batchItem);
            }

            return batchRequest;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Create Shipment Request
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="batchDataRow"></param>
        /// <param name="batchReference"></param>
        /// <param name="Params"></param>
        /// <returns>Shipment request batch item</returns>
        public ShipmentRequest GetShipmentRequest(DataRow batchDataRow, string batchReference, SerializableDictionary Params, List<BusinessRuleSetting> BusinessRuleSettings, bool batchFile, string shipper)
        {
            ShipmentRequest shipRequest = new ShipmentRequest();
            Package package = new Package();
            shipRequest.PackageDefaults = new Package();

            SetDefAttr(batchDataRow, shipRequest, Params, batchFile, batchReference, BusinessRuleSettings, shipper);
            shipRequest = SetPackageData(batchDataRow, shipRequest, Params, batchReference, BusinessRuleSettings, batchFile);

            return shipRequest;
        }

        #region Default Attribute
        /// <summary>
        /// Set all Default package Attributes
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="batchDataRow"></param>
        /// <param name="shipRequest"></param>
        private void SetDefAttr(DataRow batchDataRow, ShipmentRequest shipRequest, SerializableDictionary Params, bool batchFile, string batchReference, List<BusinessRuleSetting> BusinessRuleSettings, string shipper)
        {
            shipRequest.PackageDefaults.Shipdate = new Date(DateTime.Now);

            SetShipper(shipRequest, shipper);

            shipRequest.PackageDefaults.Consignee = SetConsigneeData(batchDataRow);

            shipRequest.PackageDefaults.ReturnAddress = SetShipFromData(batchDataRow);

            SetServiceType(batchDataRow, shipRequest, Params, batchFile, batchReference);

            SetBillingTerms(batchDataRow, shipRequest);
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Set Shipper
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="shipRequest"></param>
        /// <param name="shipper"></param>
        private void SetShipper(ShipmentRequest shipRequest, string shipper)
        {
            // Since the users are attached to ONE shipper number we will just use that one for batching
            shipRequest.PackageDefaults.Shipper = shipper;   //shipper;

        }
        #endregion

        #region Name Address Methods
        /// <summary>
        /// Set consignee data fields
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="batchDataRow"></param>
        /// <returns>consignee NameAddress Obj</returns>
        private NameAddress SetConsigneeData(DataRow batchDataRow)
        {
            NameAddress consignee = new NameAddress();

            consignee.Company = batchDataRow["ShipToCompany"].ToString();
            consignee.Contact = Tools.RemoveSpecialCharacters(batchDataRow["ShipToContact"].ToString());
            consignee.Address1 = batchDataRow["ShipToAddress1"].ToString();
            consignee.Address2 = Tools.RemoveSpecialCharacters(batchDataRow["ShipToAddress2"].ToString());
            consignee.Address3 = Tools.RemoveSpecialCharacters(batchDataRow["ShipToAddress3"].ToString());
            consignee.City = Tools.RemoveSpecialCharacters(batchDataRow["ShipToCity"].ToString());
            consignee.StateProvince = batchDataRow["ShipToState"].ToString();
            consignee.PostalCode = batchDataRow["ShipToZipcode"].ToString();
            consignee.Country = batchDataRow["ShipToCountry"].ToString();                              
            consignee.Phone = Tools.RemoveSpecialCharacters(batchDataRow["ShipToPhone"].ToString());

            // consignee.Residential = true;

            return consignee;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Set ship from data fields
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="batchDataRow"></param>
        /// <returns>ship from NameAddress Obj</returns>
        private NameAddress SetShipFromData(DataRow batchDataRow)
        {
            NameAddress shipFromData = new NameAddress();         

            shipFromData.Country = batchDataRow["ShipFromReturnCountry"].ToString();
            shipFromData.Company = Tools.RemoveSpecialCharacters(batchDataRow["ShipFromReturnCompany"].ToString());
            shipFromData.Contact = Tools.RemoveSpecialCharacters(batchDataRow["ShipFromReturnContact"].ToString());
            shipFromData.Address1 = Tools.RemoveSpecialCharacters(batchDataRow["ShipFromReturnAddress1"].ToString());
            shipFromData.Address2 = Tools.RemoveSpecialCharacters(batchDataRow["ShipFromReturnAddress2"].ToString());
            shipFromData.Address3 = Tools.RemoveSpecialCharacters(batchDataRow["ShipFromReturnAddress3"].ToString());
            shipFromData.City = Tools.RemoveSpecialCharacters(batchDataRow["ShipFromReturnCity"].ToString());
            shipFromData.StateProvince = Tools.RemoveSpecialCharacters(batchDataRow["ShipFromReturnState"].ToString());
            shipFromData.PostalCode = Tools.RemoveSpecialCharacters(batchDataRow["ShipFromReturnZipcode"].ToString());
            shipFromData.Phone = batchDataRow["ShipFromReturnPhone"].ToString();

            return shipFromData;
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Set third party data fields
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="batchDataRow"></param>
        /// <returns>3rd Party data NameAddress Obj</returns>
        private NameAddress SetThirdPartyData(DataRow batchDataRow)
        {
            NameAddress thirdPartyBillingData = new NameAddress();

            thirdPartyBillingData.Company = batchDataRow["ChangeME"].ToString();
            thirdPartyBillingData.Address1 = batchDataRow["ChangeME"].ToString();
            thirdPartyBillingData.Address2 = batchDataRow["ChangeME"].ToString();
            thirdPartyBillingData.Address3 = batchDataRow["ChangeME"].ToString();
            thirdPartyBillingData.City = batchDataRow["ChangeME"].ToString();
            thirdPartyBillingData.StateProvince = batchDataRow["ChangeME"].ToString();
            thirdPartyBillingData.PostalCode = batchDataRow["ChangeME"].ToString();
            thirdPartyBillingData.Country = batchDataRow["ChangeME"].ToString();
            thirdPartyBillingData.Phone = batchDataRow["ChangeME"].ToString();

            return thirdPartyBillingData;
        }
        #endregion

        #region Package Data Method
        /// <summary>
        /// Set all package data
        /// </summary>
        /// <param name="batchDataRow"></param>
        /// <param name="shipRequest"></param>
        /// <param name="Params"></param>
        /// <param name="batchReference"></param>
        /// <returns></returns>
